author: mark
text:
Archive 97 sync-flood duplicates from broken github-issues classifier

Triggered by bl-a2ea (now archived) shipping the projection-shape and accessor
fix. Two post-install syncs confirmed classify produces no further duplicates.

Cleanup scope (97 dups + bl-9fb4 closed by hand earlier = 98 total):
- 22 release-chore dups for shipped tags (v0.2.1, v0.3.0..v0.3.11, v0.4.0)
- 64 dups against 28 newly-created single-wrapped canonicals (issues #4..32)
  including extra clippy copies, release-plz/README trackers, and the
  meta-bug tracker issues #28/31/32 (delivered as [bl-313a], [bl-c972]
  in main and bl-a2ea in balls-github-plugin)
- 1 dup of bl-fa2a (#30 chore: release v0.5.0, not yet shipped)

Each archived task carries a note naming the canonical balls task and where
the underlying work landed (commit/tag/sibling-repo).

Note: the bl-8392 spec also called for 'bl link add ... duplicates ...' but
that command rejects closed/archived targets (bl-2fe1, bl-650b, and all
release-chore canonicals close-mirror automatically once they're matched),
so the duplicates relationship is captured in the close note instead.
